summary: Applied the control-plane recovery workflow live by enforcing the backup-store SSH path from docker-runtime-lv3 to backup-lv3, confirming the managed runtime backup timer and artifact set, mirroring the controller recovery bundle, and rerunning the restore drill against the latest archived control-plane state.

verification.observed: make syntax-check-control-plane-recovery passed on the 0.63.0 integration worktree after the OpenBao unseal and backup-store firewall fixes landed.
The committed Proxmox-host firewall play was idempotent from source commit 9899483, and backup-lv3 now allows tcp/22 from 10.10.10.20/32 for the dedicated lv3-control-plane-backup source.

verification.check: Runtime backup timer and archive set
verification.observed: docker-runtime-lv3 reports lv3-control-plane-backup.timer active and waiting, and the latest archived runtime bundle on backup-lv3 is timestamped 20260322T212846Z with step-ca, OpenBao config plus raft snapshot, Windmill database and files, mail-platform tarball, and a manifest.

backup-lv3 reran /usr/local/libexec/lv3-control-plane-restore-drill.sh and recorded result=pass at 2026-03-22T21:29:48Z against /srv/control-plane-recovery/runtime/docker-runtime-lv3/latest plus the mirrored controller bundle.

notes: The repo now enforces the previously implicit backup-lv3 guest-firewall allowance for ssh from docker-runtime-lv3, which the runtime archive push depends on.

During final live verification the docker-runtime-lv3 SSH jump path stopped answering after a forced VM stop/start, so the last runtime backup rerun was executed through the Proxmox guest agent while the restore drill and backup-store evidence were collected through the normal backup-lv3 SSH lane.
